nodes.name: Iconic Artists
nodes.description: Legendary musicians and their signature songs

items.prompt: Which artist, known as the 'King of Rock and Roll,' released 'Hound Dog' and 'Jailhouse Rock' in the 1950s?
items.explanation:
Singer, musician, actor (1935–1977)
Dubbed the 'King of Rock and Roll' — brought Black rhythm-and-blues to white mainstream audiences in the 1950s
Had 18 #1 singles on the Billboard Hot 100, including 'Heartbreak Hotel,' 'Hound Dog,' and 'Jailhouse Rock'
Graceland in Memphis is the second-most-visited private home in the U.S. after the White House
Served in the U.S. Army in Germany (1958–1960), where he met wife Priscilla
Died at age 42 at Graceland; medical examiner cited cardiac arrhythmia
First rock-and-roll artist inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame (1986, charter class)

items.prompt: Which band released 'Hey Jude' in 1968 and 'Let It Be' in 1970, becoming the best-selling music act of all time?
items.explanation:
English rock band active 1960–1970: Lennon, McCartney, Harrison, Starr
Best-selling music act of all time with estimated 600 million units sold worldwide
20 #1 singles on the Billboard Hot 100 — more than any other act
Led the 'British Invasion' of the U.S. in 1964 with their Ed Sullivan Show appearance (73 million viewers)
Pioneered studio experimentation on *Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band* (1967)
Broke up in 1970; Lennon was murdered in New York City on December 8, 1980
All four members were appointed MBE by Queen Elizabeth II in 1965

items.prompt: Which artist released the album *Thriller* in 1982, which became the best-selling album of all time?
items.explanation:
Singer, songwriter, dancer (1958–2009), known as the 'King of Pop'
*Thriller* (1982) is the best-selling album of all time with 70+ million copies sold
Invented the moonwalk, first performed on *Motown 25* TV special in 1983
Started as lead singer of the Jackson 5 at age 11; first solo #1 at age 13
13 #1 singles as a solo artist on the Billboard Hot 100
Died at age 50 from cardiac arrest caused by propofol administered by his personal physician
Super Bowl XXVII halftime show (1993) established the modern mega-halftime format

items.prompt: Which artist released 'Like a Virgin' in 1984 and 'Vogue' in 1990, becoming the best-selling female recording artist of the 20th century?
items.explanation:
Singer, actress, businesswoman (b. 1958), known as the 'Queen of Pop'
Best-selling female recording artist of all time with 300+ million records sold
'Like a Virgin' (1984) spent six weeks at #1 and became a cultural lightning rod
Held the record for most #1 hits by a female artist on the Billboard Hot 100 (12) until surpassed
Constantly reinvented her image — from 'Material Girl' to Kabbalah to electronica
Directed two feature films: *Filth and Wisdom* (2008) and *W.E.* (2011)
Inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame in 2008

items.prompt: Which artist released 'Purple Rain' in 1984 and was known for playing virtually every instrument on his recordings?
alternates: The Artist Formerly Known as Prince
items.explanation:
Singer, multi-instrumentalist, producer (1958–2016)
*Purple Rain* (1984) — both album and film — spent 24 weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200
Played 27 instruments on his debut album *For You* (1978); recorded most of his albums nearly solo
Changed his name to an unpronounceable symbol (1993–2000) in a dispute with Warner Bros. over master recordings
Guitar virtuoso: his solo on 'While My Guitar Gently Weeps' at the 2004 Rock Hall induction is legendary
Died at age 57 from an accidental fentanyl overdose at Paisley Park, his Minnesota studio compound
Won 7 Grammys and an Academy Award for Best Original Song Score (*Purple Rain*)

items.prompt: Which artist released 'Space Oddity' in 1969 and created the alter ego Ziggy Stardust?
items.explanation:
Singer, songwriter, actor (1947–2016), born David Robert Jones
Created the glam-rock alter ego Ziggy Stardust for *The Rise and Fall of Ziggy Stardust and the Spiders from Mars* (1972)
'Space Oddity' released five days before the Apollo 11 moon landing in July 1969
Reinvented his persona repeatedly: Ziggy Stardust, Aladdin Sane, the Thin White Duke
Starred in films including *The Man Who Fell to Earth* (1976) and *Labyrinth* (1986)
His Berlin Trilogy with Brian Eno (*Low*, *Heroes*, *Lodger*) pioneered art-rock and ambient music
Released final album *Blackstar* two days before his death from liver cancer; it debuted at #1

items.prompt: Which Motown artist released 'Superstition' in 1972 and signed his first record deal at age 11?
items.explanation:
Singer, songwriter, multi-instrumentalist (b. 1950), blind since shortly after birth
Signed with Motown's Tamla label at age 11 as 'Little Stevie Wonder'; first #1 hit at age 13
Won 25 Grammys — most by a solo artist at the time; three consecutive Album of the Year wins (1973–1976)
'Superstition' (1972) hit #1 and featured his signature clavinet riff
Negotiated unprecedented creative control from Motown at age 21 with *Music of My Mind* (1972)
Key campaigner for making Martin Luther King Jr. Day a federal holiday (signed into law 1983)
Plays harmonica, piano, synthesizer, drums, and bass; largely self-taught on all instruments

items.prompt: Which artist, known as the 'Queen of Soul,' recorded 'Respect' in 1967?
items.explanation:
Singer, songwriter, pianist (1942–2018), known as the 'Queen of Soul'
'Respect' (1967) — originally an Otis Redding song — became a feminist and civil rights anthem
First woman inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame (1987)
18 Grammy Awards including the first eight Grammys ever given for Best Female R&B Vocal Performance
Sang at the inaugurations of Jimmy Carter, Bill Clinton, and Barack Obama
Daughter of celebrity Baptist minister C.L. Franklin; began singing in his Detroit church as a child
Rolling Stone ranked her #1 on its list of the 100 Greatest Singers of All Time (2008, 2023)

items.prompt: Which artist released 'Blowin' in the Wind' in 1963 and won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 2016?
items.explanation:
Singer-songwriter (b. 1941), born Robert Allen Zimmerman in Duluth, Minnesota
Won the 2016 Nobel Prize in Literature 'for having created new poetic expressions within the great American song tradition'
'Blowin' in the Wind' (1963) and 'The Times They Are a-Changin'' (1964) became civil rights anthems
Shocked the folk world by going electric at the 1965 Newport Folk Festival
*Highway 61 Revisited* (1965) and *Blonde on Blonde* (1966) redefined rock lyricism
Took his stage name from the poet Dylan Thomas
Has released 39 studio albums across seven decades; still actively touring

items.prompt: Which band released 'Stairway to Heaven' and the album *Led Zeppelin IV* in 1971?
items.explanation:
English rock band active 1968–1980: Page, Plant, Jones, Bonham
'Stairway to Heaven' (1971) is the most-requested song in FM radio history, though never released as a single
Estimated 300 million albums sold worldwide; one of the best-selling music acts of all time
Pioneered hard rock and heavy metal while drawing on blues, folk, and Eastern music
Refused to release singles in the UK, insisting albums be heard as complete works
Disbanded in 1980 after drummer John Bonham's death from alcohol-related asphyxiation at age 32
Their untitled fourth album (commonly called *Led Zeppelin IV* or *Zoso*) has sold 37+ million copies

items.prompt: Which band released 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' in 1991, spearheading the grunge movement?
items.explanation:
American rock band formed in Aberdeen, Washington, 1987: Cobain, Novoselic, Grohl
'Smells Like Teen Spirit' (1991) from *Nevermind* knocked Michael Jackson off #1 and launched grunge into the mainstream
*Nevermind* has sold 30+ million copies; its baby-in-a-pool cover is one of the most iconic album covers ever
Kurt Cobain became the reluctant voice of Generation X
Their raw, lo-fi aesthetic on *In Utero* (1993) was a deliberate rejection of *Nevermind*'s polished sound
Cobain died by suicide on April 5, 1994, at age 27, joining the '27 Club'
Drummer Dave Grohl went on to found Foo Fighters, one of the biggest rock bands of the next three decades

items.prompt: Which band released *OK Computer* in 1997 and pioneered the pay-what-you-want model with *In Rainbows*?
items.explanation:
English rock band formed in Abingdon, Oxfordshire, 1985: Yorke, Greenwood, Greenwood, O'Brien, Selway
*OK Computer* (1997) explored alienation and technology; widely regarded as one of the greatest albums of all time
*Kid A* (2000) abandoned guitar rock for electronic and avant-garde textures — debuted at #1 in 21 countries
Released *In Rainbows* (2007) as a pay-what-you-want download, upending music industry distribution
Thom Yorke's falsetto and the band's layered arrangements became hugely influential on 2000s alternative rock
All five members met at Abingdon School; original name was On a Friday
Nominated for 18 Grammys, winning three

items.prompt: Which artist released *Lemonade* in 2016 and is the most-awarded artist in Grammy history?
items.explanation:
Singer, songwriter, actress, businesswoman (b. 1981), from Houston, Texas
Most Grammy wins of all time with 32 awards (surpassed Georg Solti's 31 in 2023)
Rose to fame as lead singer of Destiny's Child; went solo with *Dangerously in Love* (2003)
*Lemonade* (2016) was a surprise visual album exploring Black womanhood, infidelity, and resilience
*Renaissance* (2022) topped charts in 30+ countries and celebrated Black and queer dance music history
Super Bowl 50 halftime (2016): performed 'Formation' with Black Panther–referencing choreography
*Cowboy Carter* (2024) debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 and Country Albums chart simultaneously

items.prompt: Which guitarist performed a legendary rendition of 'The Star-Spangled Banner' at Woodstock in 1969?
items.explanation:
Guitarist, singer, songwriter (1942–1970), widely regarded as the greatest electric guitarist of all time
His Woodstock performance of 'The Star-Spangled Banner' (1969) used feedback and distortion to evoke the Vietnam War
Released only three studio albums: *Are You Experienced* (1967), *Axis: Bold as Love* (1967), *Electric Ladyland* (1968)
Was left-handed but played a right-handed Fender Stratocaster flipped upside down and restrung
Pioneered the use of feedback, wah-wah pedal, and the fuzz tone as musical elements rather than defects
Died in London at age 27 from asphyxia after ingesting barbiturates — a member of the '27 Club'
Inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame in 1992; ranked #1 guitarist by Rolling Stone

items.prompt: Which artist's rendition of 'I Will Always Love You' from *The Bodyguard* soundtrack became one of the best-selling singles of all time?
items.explanation:
Singer, actress (1963–2012), known as 'The Voice'
'I Will Always Love You' (1992) — originally a Dolly Parton song — spent 14 weeks at #1, the longest run for a female artist at the time
First artist to chart seven consecutive #1 singles on the Billboard Hot 100 (1985–1988)
*The Bodyguard* soundtrack sold 45+ million copies, making it the best-selling soundtrack of all time
Daughter of gospel singer Cissy Houston and cousin of Dionne Warwick
Won six Grammys, including Record of the Year for 'I Will Always Love You'
Drowned in a hotel bathtub in Beverly Hills at age 48; cocaine and heart disease were contributing factors

items.prompt: Which Motown artist released the concept album *What's Going On* in 1971, addressing war and social injustice?
items.explanation:
Singer, songwriter, producer (1939–1984), known as the 'Prince of Motown'
*What's Going On* (1971) addressed Vietnam, ecology, and poverty — a landmark in socially conscious pop music
'Let's Get It On' (1973) and 'Sexual Healing' (1982) established him as a master of romantic soul
Motown initially refused to release *What's Going On*; Berry Gordy relented, and it became a massive hit
'I Heard It Through the Grapevine' (1968) was Motown's biggest-selling single at the time
Shot and killed by his father, Marvin Gay Sr., on April 1, 1984 — the day before his 45th birthday
The shooting followed a domestic argument; his father received a suspended sentence after a plea deal

items.prompt: Which artist performed 'Candle in the Wind' at Princess Diana's funeral in 1997, producing the best-selling single in UK chart history?
items.explanation:
Singer, pianist, composer (b. 1947), born Reginald Kenneth Dwight
'Candle in the Wind 1997' sold 33+ million copies, making it the best-selling physical single of all time
Longtime songwriting partnership with lyricist Bernie Taupin, beginning in 1967 via a shared audition
Composed the music for Disney's *The Lion King* (1994), winning the Academy Award for 'Can You Feel the Love Tonight'
Over 300 million records sold; five #1 albums on the Billboard 200
Knighted in 1998; became Sir Elton Hercules John, CBE
Farewell Yellow Brick Road tour (2018–2023) grossed $939 million, the highest-grossing tour of all time at its conclusion
items.cardFront: 'Candle in the Wind' / *Goodbye Yellow Brick Road*

items.prompt: Which band released *Rumours* in 1977, recorded while its members were going through romantic breakups with each other?
items.explanation:
British-American rock band formed in London, 1967; classic lineup: Buckingham, Nicks, C. McVie, J. McVie, Fleetwood
*Rumours* (1977) has sold 40+ million copies; recorded during simultaneous breakups between couples in the band
Spent 31 weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200 and won the Grammy for Album of the Year (1978)
Originally a British blues band led by Peter Green; completely reinvented after Buckingham and Nicks joined in 1975
'Dreams' became the band's only #1 single; went viral again in 2020 via a TikTok skateboarding video
Stevie Nicks was inducted into the Rock Hall both as a member of Fleetwood Mac (1998) and as a solo artist (2019)
Band name combines the surnames of drummer Mick Fleetwood and bassist John McVie

items.prompt: Which band released *The Dark Side of the Moon* in 1973, the album that spent over 900 weeks on the Billboard 200?
items.explanation:
English rock band formed in London, 1965: Barrett, Waters, Gilmour, Wright, Mason
*The Dark Side of the Moon* (1973) spent 917+ weeks on the Billboard 200 — longest chart run of any album ever
*The Wall* (1979) produced 'Another Brick in the Wall (Part 2),' their only #1 single
Pioneered concept albums, quadraphonic sound, and elaborate live shows with inflatables and projection
Original frontman Syd Barrett left in 1968 due to mental illness, inspiring the albums *Wish You Were Here* and *The Wall*
The prism cover art of *The Dark Side of the Moon*, designed by Storm Thorgerson, is one of the most recognized album covers ever
Roger Waters and David Gilmour's feud split the band; they reunited briefly for Live 8 in 2005

items.prompt: Which Jamaican artist released 'No Woman, No Cry' and brought reggae music to a worldwide audience?
items.explanation:
Singer, songwriter, guitarist (1945–1981), born in Nine Mile, Jamaica
Brought reggae to the global mainstream as frontman of Bob Marley and the Wailers
*Legend* (1984), a posthumous compilation, is the best-selling reggae album of all time (25+ million copies)
Devout Rastafarian whose music fused reggae with messages of Pan-Africanism and social justice
Shot in a politically motivated assassination attempt at his Kingston home in 1976; performed two days later
Died at age 36 from acral lentiginous melanoma that spread from under his toenail
Received a state funeral in Jamaica attended by both the prime minister and the opposition leader
